text: Hi, I am Dr. Şaban Beyazpınar, in Turkey.
This case is an explanatory video of laparoscopic machial diverticulaectomy with linear stapler during laparoscopic appendectomy surgery.
Our clinic case is a thirty-eight years old Turkish male patient.
Since three years there have been occasionally abdominal pains and nausea.
Intraoperative evaluation, right lower quadrant head pain and tenderness as well as rebound tenderness. Acute phase reactants are high.
Local abdominal ultrasonography revealed local peritonitis including local ileus minimally fluid around the intestine and suspicious diverticula. Lower abdominal CT revealed diverticula.
It was decided that appendicitis or the verticillitis could be diagnosed with acute abdomen and operation was obtained.
The surgeon places laparoscopic ports, including a 10mm umbilical port and a 5mm port in the left lower quadrant, operating under a pressure of 10mmHg.
The patient's position, Drendelenburg, in the spine position. The patient's position, the location of the ports and the location of the surgical team are showing in the diagrams.
The laparoscopic camera is introduced into the abdominal cavity, providing a view of the internal structures as the surgeon begins exploration.
The surgeon places a 5mm port in the left lower quadrant and another 5mm port in the suprapubic area, preparing for the appendectomy procedure.
The surgeon reveals and identifies the appendix using ligature, then cuts the mass of the appendix and releases it from surrounding tissue.
The surgeon erases the radix of the appendix, completing the removal of the appendix from its base.
The surgeon uses an end-loop with a number zero V-create to ligate the appendix twice at the top, completing the appendectomy with visible ligatures securing the structure.
The surgeon removes the appendix through the ten-millimeter camera port, completing the extraction process.

The surgeon explores the abdominal cavity and small intestine using a camera, detecting a Meckel's diverticulum in the terminal ileum. A five-millimeter port in the bottom left quadrant is removed and replaced with a twelve-millimeter port at the same incision site.
The surgeon excises the Meckel's diverticulum using a linear laparoscopic stapler, completing the removal of the diverticulum.
The excised Meckel's diverticulum is removed from the abdominal cavity through the twelve-millimeter port in the left lower quadrant.
The surgeon completes the operation by controlling bleeding and aspirating fluids from the abdominal cavity.

Patient was discharged with minimal pain on the first postoperative day. On the seventh day, port's tooth were taken. Surgical complication was not observed. One month later, it was seen again. Nutrition and health problem did not occur. He got rid of his pain. Pathology revealed gastric mucosa within the inner surface make us diverticulum and acute sporadic appendicitis for appendix. Thank you for all.
